name: Bigos- Hunter's Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Polish sausage links, cut into 1/2-inch cubes
1 pound Polish white sausage with garlic, cut into 1/2-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse sauerkraut with cold water and drain well.
2. In a large stockpot, combine sauerkraut, ham or pork, bay leaves, mushrooms, peppercorns, allspice, and salt.
3. Add 6 cups of broth or water. Cook, uncovered, over medium heat for 15 minutes.
4. Cover and simmer over low heat for 45 minutes.
5. Remove meat from the pot and let it cool.
6. Place cabbage in a large saucepan. Add remaining 5 cups of broth or water. Bring to boil.
7. Cook, uncovered, over medium heat for 1 hour or until cabbage is tender. Add to sauerkraut mixture.
8. Remove cooked meat from the refrigerator and cut into 1/2-inch cubes. Melt butter in a large skillet.
9. Add cooked meat, smoked sausage, and white sausage to the skillet. Sauté over medium heat for 10 minutes or until browned.
10. Add to sauerkraut mixture.
11. In the same skillet, saute bacon until crisp. Drain on paper towels and add to the sauerkraut mixture.
12. Cover; cook over low heat for 1 hour or longer.
13. Remove and discard bay leaves. Serve hot. Makes 12 to 14 servings. Use only meat with large bones so they can be removed easily. The secret of old-time Bigos is that it gets better as it's reheated on successive days, peaking at the 6th or 7th day. Store covered in the refrigerator between uses.
